purchaseTriggers: Sleep problems — specifically inability to fall asleep or stay asleep despite trying other remedies
Muscle cramps and restless leg syndrome, particularly after exercise or at night
Doctor or cardiologist recommendation for the glycinate form specifically
Looking for a gentler magnesium form after experiencing GI distress from oxide or citrate forms
Chronic low magnesium levels confirmed by blood work
complaints: Tablets are large and require a full glass of water to swallow comfortably
Inconsistent effectiveness — a minority of reviewers report no noticeable difference after 6 weeks
Subtle smell when opening the bottle that surprises first-time buyers
Taking 2–4 tablets per dose feels excessive compared to single-capsule competitors
Higher price per serving than oxide-based alternatives, which deters price-sensitive buyers
unspokenFeatures: The TRAACS chelation mechanism is mentioned positively in reviews by informed buyers but is not visually explained in any listing image — reviewers who understand it become advocates but most shoppers skip past the text

Third-party testing is cited as a trust reason in multiple reviews ('I trust this brand because it is independently tested') but the certification image is buried in the last slide where most mobile shoppers never reach
The glycinate vs oxide bioavailability comparison is the single most convincing clinical argument and does not appear as a standalone visual anywhere in the listing
The healthcare professional endorsement pattern ('my cardiologist recommended this brand by name') is a high-converting social proof angle not represented in the imagery
emotionalDrivers: Distrust of pharmaceutical sleep aids — buyers want something evidence-backed but not a drug
Fear of deficiency — reviewers cite feeling 'chronically low' and wanting to correct a measurable health gap rather than a vague wellness aspiration
Identity as a health-conscious consumer who researches rather than accepts generic options — the TRAACS patent is a signal that rewards this identity
Relief after a long search — the dominant emotional tone across positive reviews is relief at having found something that finally works after trying multiple alternatives
